experiment: E1 economics demo, MULTI-STEP agent scale (paper/log.md 2026-07-04 wave 5)
agent: inbox-agent: per run, 3 real gpt-5.4-mini calls (classify, priority, summarize) + 1 local tool (lookup) + python glue; 60-ticket corpus

artifacts.summarize: 08a5143b... (tree @4096 buckets — the 20%-deterministic free-text step compiled to its single witnessed behavior per input; first attempt at 1024 buckets honestly FAILED the gate at train 0.733)

ratios_measured.marginal_cost: 188 usd-micros/run -> 0; recording spend (11274 usd-micros) amortizes after ~60 runs
ratios_measured.systems_finding: artifact execution is <1ms (manifests); the compiled path's 173ms mean is python glue + HTTP + per-request wasm instantiation — once model calls compile away, the runtime around them becomes the bottleneck; in-process embedding of artifacts is the recorded path to the next order of magnitude

determinism_measured.protocol: 20 tickets x 2 passes through BOTH recorders on identical traffic (auto proxy raw chat bodies + SDK agent-shaped spans)
determinism_measured.sdk_view: 78.8% (126/160 effectful spans incl. the tool call)

honesty_caveats: designed 60-ticket corpus; the agent is single-turn per step (no inter-step model feedback loops)

all three artifacts compile the witnessed distribution (--holdout 0); guards abstain beyond calibration; the 20%-deterministic summarize step is pinned to one recorded behavior per input — statistical acceptance for divergent outputs remains the recorded open question
total wave-5 recording spend ~18,800 usd-micros (~$0.019: 180 baseline + 120 proxy-leg calls), owner-direct, measured via span attrs
